edge: Language models are trained using tokens, and different tokens have distinct loss patterns during training.
RHO-1 is a new type of language model that employs Selective Language Modeling (SLM) compared to traditional LMs.
RHO-1 selectively trains on useful tokens that align with the desired distribution by scoring tokens using a reference model.
Continual pretraining on various corpus sizes enables RHO-1 to achieve significant improvements in accuracy and performance.
RHO-1 achieves state-of-the-art results on the MATH dataset, matching DeepSeekMath with only 3% of the pretraining tokens.

SLM is inspired by the practice of the reference model in document-level filtering. The pipeline of SLM involves training a reference model on a curated dataset and then assessing the loss of each token to selectively train the language model.
SLM involves a token-level data selection process, where tokens with high excess loss are focused on during training to enhance the model's efficiency.
The reference model is trained on a high-quality dataset and then used to score each token's loss in a corpus to guide the training of the language model.
The reference model is used to assess the loss of each token within the pretraining corpus, allowing for selective training of the language model based on token scores.

The token-level data selection process involves focusing on tokens with high excess loss between the training and reference model, as they are considered more learnable and aligned with the desired distribution.

Tokens with high excess loss are deemed more learnable, indicating they are better aligned with the desired distribution, and are therefore selectively trained during the language model training process.

In this paper, we propose using Selective Language Modeling(SLM) to train RHO-1, which selects more suitable tokens for the current pretraining stage.
Our experiments and analysis in the fields of mathematics and general have demonstrated the effectiveness of the SLM method, emphasizing the importance of token level in the LLM pretraining process.
